executive_summary: GO — Restaurant food waste prediction SaaS scores 72.9/100 weighted, passing all GO conditions with no knockouts; strong unit economics (LTV/CAC 8.97x) and validated problem (75/100) support market entry, though competitive incumbency (Restaurant365 $283M) and POS integration gatekeeping require careful execution.
score_reasoning: Weighted score 72.9/100 — Problem: 75/100 x 0.30 = 22.5 (well-documented pain with 22 complaint threads, 7 workarounds, 14 paid alternatives). Market: 72/100 x 0.25 = 18.0 ($3.1B TAM, $24M SOM, 11.1% CAGR, 3+ early adopter segments with 1.87M+ reachable members). Competitive: 62/100 x 0.15 = 9.3 (16+ competitors validate market but R365 $283M and Winnow $36.6M create moderate incumbency; clear gaps in predictive AI + supplier integration for SMBs). BizModel: 82/100 x 0.20 = 16.4 (LTV/CAC 8.97x, 2.23-month payback, subscription model validated by 6+ funded restaurant SaaS companies). Risk: 67/100 x 0.10 = 6.7 (moderate risk; technically feasible, light regulation, good timing, but POS gatekeeping and incumbent expansion are top threats). Knockouts: None.

data.executive_summary: GO with 72.9/100 weighted score. Strong unit economics (LTV/CAC 8.97x, 2.23-month payback) and a well-validated pain point (75/100) support market entry into restaurant food waste prediction SaaS, though the competitive landscape requires differentiation through predictive AI and supplier integration for SMB restaurants.
key_strengths: Unit economics are excellent: LTV/CAC of 8.97x with 2.23-month payback at $299/mo, resilient across all sensitivity scenarios (CAC+20%, churn+20%, price-20% all remain viable)
Problem is real and well-documented: 22 unique complaint threads, 7 distinct workarounds, 14 paid alternatives — restaurant operators actively spend on this problem

Subscription model thoroughly validated: 6+ funded restaurant SaaS companies (Restaurant365 $1B valuation, Toast public, MarketMan, Apicbase, ClearCOGS, Owner.com) prove the model works in this segment
Clear market gaps align with the idea: predictive AI forecasting for SMBs, supplier integration, and affordable pricing are the top 3 unmet needs across competitor reviews
Strong early adopter identifiability: 3+ segments with concrete reach channels (r/KitchenConfidential 1.8M, NRA Show 57K, r/restaurantowners 52K)
key_concerns: Dominant incumbent threat: Restaurant365 ($283M funding, $1B valuation, 318 G2 reviews) already has Smart Prep/Smart Ordering AI features — could expand to cover predictive waste + supplier integration

POS integration gatekeeping: Toast requires partner agreement vetting across compliance, privacy, security, and legal teams before granting write API access — rejection or delay blocks go-to-market for a large customer segment

ML cold-start problem: predictive forecasting requires sufficient historical data per restaurant — early predictions may be inaccurate, causing churn in a community where word-of-mouth reputation matters
Competitive opportunity score is lowest sub-dimension (6/20) due to Restaurant365 and Winnow entrenchment — market is validated but not wide open
Restaurant thin margins (3-9% net profit) create price sensitivity — must demonstrate clear ROI to justify $299/mo spend
critical_assumptions: Monthly churn rate of 5% based on B2B SaaS SMB benchmarks (UserMotion 2024: 4.2% avg) — adjusted for restaurant SMB segment with moderate switching costs
CAC of $500 blended (FirstPageSage Hospitality SMB: $907, ClickStrike: $280-$600) — assumes content marketing + paid search mix
Gross margin of 75% for pure SaaS without hardware (industry standard 70-85%)
SOM of $24M based on 3% of $806M SAM — top-down estimate, not bottom-up validated
Price point of $299/mo based on competitive benchmark positioning, not direct customer willingness-to-pay testing

next_steps.action: Secure Toast POS partner application and begin API integration approval process immediately — this is the critical path dependency with longest lead time
next_steps.timeframe: 1-2 weeks to apply, 2-3 months for approval
next_steps.rationale: Toast is the dominant US restaurant POS; without integration, a large portion of the addressable market is unreachable. Read-only access is sufficient for MVP (historical sales data for predictions).

next_steps.action: Build a landing page targeting r/KitchenConfidential (1.8M members) and r/restaurantowners (52K members) with a waitlist, measuring conversion rate and signup volume
next_steps.rationale: Validates willingness-to-pay and demand intensity at the $299/mo price point with the largest reachable early adopter community. Target: 200+ signups in 14 days.

next_steps.action: Run 10-15 customer discovery interviews with multi-location restaurant operators (5-50 units) to validate the $299/mo price point and prioritize features (predictive forecasting vs. supplier integration)
next_steps.rationale: Price point is based on competitive benchmark, not direct customer input. Multi-location operators are the highest-value early adopter segment with clearest pain.

next_steps.action: Prototype a demand forecasting model using publicly available restaurant transaction datasets to establish baseline prediction accuracy before customer onboarding
next_steps.rationale: Addresses the ML cold-start risk — demonstrating minimum viable accuracy (e.g., within 15% of actual demand) before first pilot reduces early churn risk.

next_steps.action: Monitor Restaurant365 product announcements and acquisition activity — if R365 announces predictive waste or supplier integration features, accelerate go-to-market or consider partnership
next_steps.rationale: R365 is the dominant incumbent threat. Early warning signals from competitive intelligence should trigger strategic response.

validation_experiments.experiment: Landing page + waitlist targeting restaurant communities (r/KitchenConfidential, r/restaurantowners, RestaurantOwner.com forum) with clear value prop: 'Predict food waste before it happens — save $333/day'
validation_experiments.success_metric: 200+ email signups in 14 days with >3% visitor-to-signup conversion rate
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: Demand intensity and willingness to engage at the proposed price point ($299/mo shown on page)

validation_experiments.experiment: Pre-sale interviews with 10 multi-location restaurant operators offering 50% founding member discount ($149/mo for first year) in exchange for early access and data sharing
validation_experiments.success_metric: 3+ operators sign LOI or pre-pay at $149/mo; 7+ express strong interest with specific pain points matching the value prop
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: Willingness to pay, priority feature set (prediction vs. supplier integration), and data access feasibility (POS type, data quality, history length)

validation_experiments.experiment: Cold-start prediction accuracy test: build baseline demand forecasting model using open restaurant datasets (e.g., Kaggle restaurant revenue prediction, NYC restaurant inspection data + weather data) and measure prediction accuracy
validation_experiments.success_metric: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) < 20% on held-out test data using only historical sales + weather + day-of-week features
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: Technical feasibility of the core ML prediction engine; addresses the cold-start risk identified by Risk department

validation_experiments.experiment: Toast API partner application submission and integration prototype with read-only access to validate data extraction pipeline
validation_experiments.success_metric: Partner application accepted within 90 days; successful extraction of historical sales data from 1+ test restaurant via API
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: POS integration feasibility — the #2 killer risk; validates that data access is achievable without write permissions

evidence: No knockout conditions triggered: Problem 75 >= 40, Market 72 >= 40, Risk 67 >= 30, no two departments below 45
6+ funded restaurant SaaS precedents validate the subscription model: Restaurant365 ($1B), Toast (public), MarketMan, Apicbase, ClearCOGS, Owner.com
